zotero.abstractNote: Bayesian Optimization (BO) is a powerful framework for global optimization of expensive-to-evaluate black-box functions. While standard BO assumes continuous input spaces, many real-world problems involve mixed-variable spaces, containing continuous, discrete, and categorical variables. Existing approaches for mixed-variable BO often rely on simple heuristics or are limited to specific variable types. In this paper, we propose a novel approach for mixed-variable BO that leverages a latent space representation. We map the mixed-variable space into a continuous latent space using a variational autoencoder (VAE) and perform BO in this latent space. We demonstrate that our approach outperforms existing methods on a variety of synthetic and real-world benchmarks.
zotero.conferenceName: International Joint Conference on Artificial Intelligence
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the Twenty-Ninth International Joint Conference on Artificial Intelligence (IJCAI-20)
